channel.title: aidemo blog
channel.description: Practical, evidence-backed writing on product demo videos and demo automation.

item.title: AI demo video generators: what the label actually covers
item.description: Four unrelated tools rank for 'AI demo video generator,' and two never output a video of your product. A mechanism map so you buy the right object.

item.title: AI voiceover for demo videos: quality, cost, and workflow
item.description: AI voiceover per minute is settled and cheap. The real questions: which TTS gives you consistency and one-line re-renders, and where a human voice still wins.

item.title: App preview videos: hitting App Store and Play specs without a reshoot
item.description: Apple wants an uploaded H.264 file at 886 x 1920; Google Play wants a YouTube link. The exact cross-store specs, and how to hit them without a reshoot.

item.title: Arcade vs Navattic vs Storylane: picking an interactive demo platform
item.description: They rank on the same grids, but Navattic clones your DOM, Arcade stitches screenshots, and Storylane does both. Pick by capture engine, not feature count.

item.title: Automated product demo videos: regenerate, don't re-record
item.description: Four unrelated machines ship under one label. Only one makes 'the product changed' and 'the demo is current' the same event.

item.title: Automating product screenshots for docs and marketing
item.description: The complete stills playbook: one YAML for every screenshot, a pull request that recaptures them on a schedule, and a visual regression test you get for free.

item.title: Camtasia alternatives for software demos and tutorials
item.description: TechSmith retired Camtasia's perpetual license, and the timeline was always the real bill. The alternatives, sorted by who does the editing.

item.title: Letting a coding agent make your demo video
item.description: A coding agent can explore your app and write the demo storyboard. Driving the browser live is the one thing it must not do, and here is exactly why.

item.title: Captions on demo videos: the accessibility case and the numbers
item.description: Most demo views start muted, so the caption is the demo, not the soundtrack: the timing, the formats, and the WCAG rule that decide whether the words land.

item.title: Demo video script template: problem, walkthrough, proof
item.description: Narration runs about 2.5 words a second, so a demo's runtime is a word budget. Grab a copy-paste script skeleton and two worked scripts, counted line by line.

item.title: Rendering demo videos in CI: a GitHub Actions walkthrough
item.description: GitHub's runner ships Chrome but not ffmpeg. Here is what a video-rendering job actually needs, how to trigger it, and where the file should land.

item.title: Demos as code: commit the storyboard, render the video
item.description: A recording is a performance you own and maintain. A spec is text you can diff, review, regenerate, and hand to an agent. That decides everything downstream.

item.title: Deterministic browser automation for video capture
item.description: A test that flakes just reruns. A demo that flakes ships the stutter. The eight-variable checklist for making one browser flow render the same pixels twice.

item.title: Does video actually increase conversion? Tracing the 80% claim
item.description: The famous 80% conversion lift traces to one 2011 A/B test. Here is what a 35,000-page analysis and your LCP budget actually say about video.

item.title: How long should a demo video be? The retention math
item.description: Most demos run longer than they should, and that is an editing problem. Here is the target length for every placement, plus what to cut first.

item.title: How to make a product demo video (that people finish)
item.description: The playbook for a demo people actually finish: script first, record second, with the word, scene, and length budgets that decide every second.

item.title: How to record your screen in high quality: the settings that matter
item.description: Blurry text, a laggy cursor, the wrong frame rate: high quality is a handful of capture settings, each with one number and a one-line fix.

item.title: Interactive product demos vs video demos: when each wins
item.description: One is a program you drive, the other a file that plays anywhere. Where clickable tours beat video, where video wins, and the two costs listicles skip.

item.title: Loom alternatives for product demos after the Atlassian era
item.description: Loom is async video messaging that Atlassian now owns, not a product-demo tool. Sort the alternatives by which of Loom's jobs you are actually replacing.

item.title: Multi-language product demo videos from one recording
item.description: Localizing a demo doesn't mean re-shooting it five times. Keep one recorded take, swap the words, and do the honest math on where each locale really costs you.

item.title: Open-source demo video tools, scored by license and install reality
item.description: Not everything on GitHub is open source. Cap, vhs, Remotion, Revideo, editly, and aidemo, scored on OSI compliance, install friction, and what the pixels are.

item.title: Personalized demo videos at scale: the template-render pattern
item.description: A merge field with the prospect's name is not a demo that shows their data. Three depths of personalization, priced honestly for a fifty-prospect batch.

item.title: What makes a screen recording look professional
item.description: The polish people call professional is five named transforms with parameters, not a knack. Here is what each one does and why a bad zoom is a recompose.

item.title: README GIFs that update themselves
item.description: A 20-second demo GIF can weigh 18 MB, past GitHub's 10 MB ceiling. The format rules, the real size math, and a recapture loop that keeps it honest.

item.title: How to record just one browser tab (three ways, one clean result)
item.description: Recording one browser tab is picking a capture surface, not cropping your desktop. Three ways to do it, scored on fidelity, what leaks, and repeatability.

item.title: SaaS demo video examples: what the best ones do differently
item.description: Six real SaaS demo videos from Vercel, Notion, Loom, Linear, Warp, and Retool, read with a stopwatch and a transcript instead of a mood board.

item.title: Screen Studio alternatives for Windows, Linux, and CI
item.description: Screen Studio is macOS-only and subscription-only now. Here is the honest, per-platform map of what replaces it on Windows, Linux, and in CI.

item.title: Video-as-code tools: Remotion, Revideo, editly, vhs — and real-UI capture
item.description: Remotion and Revideo draw every frame, editly stitches clips, vhs replays a terminal, real-UI capture films your product. Five models, one map.

item.title: Video documentation for software: when the medium earns its keep
item.description: The evidence-based case for video as a documentation medium: where a clip beats a paragraph, where it quietly loses, and how to route each doc job.

item.title: Video marketing for software: one recording, every channel
item.description: Your buyers sit on nine surfaces; your budget is for one recording. The channel-by-channel spec that turns a single demo into every placement it needs.
